title: Session state decision_log schema mismatch
observation: Requirements subagent generated decision_log entries with fields (id: DL-xxx, decision) instead of required schema (id: Dxxx, agent, title, choice)
expected: decision_log entries should use D001 id pattern and include agent, title, choice fields per validate-session-state.mjs
root_cause: Subagent prompt did not include the exact decision_log schema; agent used its own format
recommendation: Include decision_log JSON schema example in Requirements subagent prompt

title: Azure AD B2C used for greenfield 2026 project
observation: Requirements agent defaulted to Azure AD B2C for CIAM despite it being unavailable for new customers since May 2025
expected: Agent should use Microsoft Entra External ID for new greenfield projects
root_cause: Agent training data includes older B2C references; no guardrail for deprecated service detection
recommendation: Add Entra External ID as default CIAM option in azure-defaults skill; add deprecation check for B2C

title: RFQ Table 2 services not faithfully preserved in functional inventory
observation: Core Capabilities table replaced cloud services with business features and merged storage types, losing 1:1 traceability to RFQ Table 2
expected: All 15 RFQ Table 2 services should appear as separate entries with original volumetrics
root_cause: Agent interpreted requirements at business level rather than preserving the RFQ cloud service inventory verbatim
recommendation: Prompt should explicitly instruct: preserve RFQ Table 2 as a faithful cloud services inventory table, separate from business capabilities

title: Duplicate Executive Summary H2 heading in architecture assessment
observation: Architecture subagent generated two H2 Executive Summary headings (💎 and 📋 variants)
expected: Single 💎 Executive Summary H2 per artifact template
root_cause: Agent template instructions and prompt H2 list diverged
recommendation: Align prompt H2 list with artifact template exactly

title: Redis Enterprise E50 retires within contract term
observation: Architecture selected Redis Enterprise E50 with 3-year RI, but Enterprise E50 retires March 31, 2027 — within the 3-year contract window (2026-2029)
expected: Agent should check service retirement dates before recommending long-term commitments
root_cause: No deprecation/retirement checking performed during service selection
recommendation: Add retirement date verification step to Architect agent for any 3-year commitment services

title: APIM Standard v2 does not support zone redundancy
observation: Architecture claimed zone-redundant critical path but selected APIM Standard v2 which has no AZ support
expected: APIM Premium v2 or Premium classic required for zone-redundant SLA composition
root_cause: Agent selected cheaper SKU without verifying zone-redundancy compatibility
recommendation: Add SKU-to-zone-redundancy validation in architecture assessment

title: AKS 2-node baseline cannot cover 3 availability zones
observation: Architecture stated 3-AZ spread but only provisioned 2 user nodes, leaving one zone empty at baseline
expected: Minimum 3 user nodes (1 per AZ) for true 3-zone resilience
root_cause: Cost optimization overrode reliability design; node count < zone count not validated
recommendation: Enforce min_node_count >= AZ_count validation in architecture review

title: Resource inventory omitted APIM from 15-service estate
observation: 07-resource-inventory.md listed 14 of 15 services — APIM was missing despite being a critical production component documented in all prior steps
expected: All 15 RFQ Table 2 services should appear in the resource inventory
root_cause: As-Built agent categorized APIM as an edge/networking concern and did not create a separate inventory row for it
recommendation: As-Built agent should cross-reference resource inventory against the AVM matrix or implementation plan resource table

title: GDPR compliance matrix lacked RFQ Section 4.3 clause-by-clause mapping
observation: Compliance matrix used broad GDPR statements rather than mapping each specific RFQ 4.3 clause (logs, backups, metadata, telemetry, caching, indexing, remote support)
expected: Each RFQ Section 4.3 data residency clause should map to a specific Azure control
root_cause: Step 1 expanded the requirements but Step 7 summarized instead of preserving the detailed clause structure
recommendation: Compliance matrix should directly reference RFQ clause numbers and map each to implementation evidence

title: DR plan did not cover APIM and identity configuration recovery
observation: Backup-DR plan documented RTO for APIM as critical but had no export/import procedure for APIs, policies, products, or Entra External ID configuration
expected: All services with declared RTO must have documented backup and restore procedures
root_cause: As-Built agent focused on data services (PostgreSQL, Redis, Blob) and omitted control-plane state for PaaS services
recommendation: DR plan should include APIOps export/import for APIM and Graph API backup for identity configuration
